title: Africa’s Biggest IPOSparks Investor Frenzy in Dangote’s Nigeria
summary: Africa’s richest person is sparking investor fever across Nigeria. Dangote's $40 billion IPO has people from all walks of life opening trading accounts out of FOMO, Ruth Olurounbi explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Japan’s $2.3 Trillion Investment Plan Raises Fresh JGB Concerns
summary: Bond strategists are warning that Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s $2.3 trillion investment plan risks putting fresh pressure on Japan’s government debt market.

title: Hawkish BOJ Member Calls for Rate Hike Every Few Months
summary: The Bank of Japan needs to raise interest rates every few months and should quicken the pace in the face of stronger inflation risks, said Naoki Tamura, one of the most hawkish board members.

title: Stocks Rally as Micron Revives AI Bets, Oil Slumps: Markets Wrap
summary: Global stocks rebounded from a two-day slide as Micron Technology Inc.’s blowout sales forecast reignited enthusiasm for the artificial-intelligence trade and Brent oil erased all of its wartime gains.

title: H.B. Fuller Nears £628 Million Deal for UK Medtech AMS
summary: H.B. Fuller Co. is close to an agreement to acquire Advanced Medical Solutions Group Plc in a deal valuing the equity of the British maker of tissue-healing medical products at £628 million ($827 million).

title: FTSE 100 Set to Fall as Brent Slides Below $73
Australia’s Housing Slump Wipes $128 Billion Off Top Two Markets
summary: Australia’s housing downturn has wiped A$185 billion ($128 billion) off the value of the nation’s top two markets so far this quarter, a result that’s likely to weigh on the wealth effect that underpins consumer spending across the economy.

title: Brent Oil Erases Wartime Gains as Hormuz Reopening Boosts Supply
summary: Brent oil erased all of its wartime gains after flows through the Strait of Hormuz ramped up following progress on a US-Iran peace deal.

title: Oil Erases Wartime Gains as Hormuz Stockpiles Hit Market
summary: Goldman economist Farouk Soussa talks about why the Strait of Hormuz doesn't need to come back to 100% for the full amount of oil from the Gulf to come back into the market, and the longer-term outlook for crude amid the US-Iran negotiations. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Venezuela Hit by Massive Earthquakes
summary: Venezuela declared a state of emergency after massive back-to-back earthquakes toppled buildings in Caracas, knocked down power lines and devastated the capital’s main airport. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Syfe On Growing APAC Retail Investors
summary: Dhruv Arora, Founder & CEO at Syfe, discusses the investment platform's expansion plans and retail investor sentiment. He speaks on "Bloomberg: The China Show." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: StanChart's Liu: PBOC Tightening Unlikely
summary: Becky Liu, Head of Greater China Strategy and Asia Rates at Standard Chartered Bank, discusses China's central bank policy shift. She speaks on "Bloomberg: The China Show."  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Cal-Maine, Others Poised to Settle DOJ Egg Pricing Probe
summary: Cal-Maine Foods Inc. and other egg suppliers are close to resolving an investigation by the US Justice Department and a bipartisan group of states into alleged illegal price coordination, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Bitcoin’s $10 Billion Options Expiry Risks Deepening Selloff
summary: Bitcoin is facing a massive options expiry that risks putting more pressure on a market already struggling with fading institutional demand and macroeconomic headwinds.

title: PBOC Plans Overnight Reverse Repo in Next Stage of Policy Shift
summary: China’s central bank is introducing an overnight tenor into its open-market operations, a key step toward reshaping how it steers short-term borrowing costs.

title: Palm Oil Posts Biggest Drop in a Month as Crude Erases War Gains
summary: Palm oil futures posted the biggest drop in more than a month, tracking declines in crude and soy oil prices, while a strengthening ringgit added to demand headwinds.

title: Micron Revives AI Trade; SK Hynix Seeks US Listing | The Asia Trade 6/25/2026
summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: How Warsh Sank the Debasement Trade
summary: It’s on its last legs, while Micron is showing that there’s still some life left in chips.

title: Mideast Oil Revival Gathers Pace as Qatar Sells Crude to Asia
summary: Qatar has joined Persian Gulf nations in reviving crude sales, as regional producers crank up activity following the US-Iran interim peace deal.

title: Japan’s 20-Year Bond Sale Sees Lowest Demand Since May 2025 Rout
summary: Japan’s 20-year bond auction drew the weakest demand since the market upheaval just over a year ago as worries about inflation and fiscal policy came back to the fore.

title: Copper Climbs as Lower Dollar, AI Rally Counter Rate Concerns
summary: Copper edged higher, after touching a seven-week low on Wednesday, as a weakening dollar and an artificial intelligence stock rally buoyed sentiment for the metal used in electrification.

title: Adnoc LNG Tanker Appears in Persian Gulf as Transparency Returns
summary: A liquefied natural gas tanker owned by Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. began sending a signal from within the Persian Gulf, as more vessels broadcast their journeys and intentions in recent days following an interim peace deal between the US and Iran.

title: Alibaba Slides to 16-Month Low After Anthropic’s AI Accusations
summary: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. shares slid to a 16-month low in Hong Kong after Anthropic PBC accused the Chinese technology giant of “illicitly” accessing its artificial intelligence model.

title: Australian Hiring Rebounds in May, Pushing Down Unemployment
summary: Australian hiring rebounded in May, reversing the previous month’s losses, while the jobless rate edged lower in a validation of the Reserve Bank’s view the labor market still remains tight.

title: Japan’s Kioxia Plans to Offer US Depositary Shares in Spring
summary: Kioxia Holdings Corp. intends to offer US depositary receipts in the spring of 2027 to take advantage of runaway investor demand for exposure to AI-related semiconductor shares.

title: Venezuela Hit by Massive Back-to-Back Earthquakes
summary: Venezuela has declared a state of emergency after massive back-to-back earthquakes toppled buildings in Caracas, knocked down power lines and devastated the capital’s main airport. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hong Kong Dollar Nears Weak End on Low Volatility, Cheap Rates
summary: The Hong Kong dollar is heading toward the weak end of its fixed trading range as multi-year low volatility and cheap borrowing costs make it easier for traders to short the currency against the greenback.

title: Gold Slumps Below $4,000 as Stronger Dollar, Rate Outlook Weigh
summary: Gold extended a decline, a day after a resurgent US dollar and the prospect of higher interest rates pushed the metal below $4,000 an ounce for the first time since November.

title: SK Hynix Surges 12% as US Listing Plan Boosts Valuation Hopes
summary: SK Hynix Inc. shares surged after the memory chipmaker unveiled its plan for a $29 billion US listing, a move that analysts viewed as boosting valuations through capacity expansion and greater foreign investor access.
